title: Microgilia minutiflora
intro: Microgilia minutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, commonly known as the "minuscule-flowered microgilia" due to its small, delicate flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Microgilia minutiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Microgilia minutiflora is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound healing.
